senses.gloss: to enter, go in, come in
to penetrate, to pierce
to befall, to seize, to come over (someone) (e.g. of a doubt, joy, etc.)
to take up (a profession, etc.), to start
to drop in on, to come to see, to call on, to visit
to consummate a marriage, to cohabit, to sleep

examples.text: […] مِنْ نِسَائِكُمُ اللَّاتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ […]
examples.english: [born] of the wives whom you have gone into—but if you have not gone into them there is no sin upon you

senses.gloss: to acquire
to join, to convert to

examples.text: وَرَأَيْتَ ٱلنَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ ٱللّٰهِ أَفْوَاجًا
examples.english: and you see the people entering God's religion in throngs

senses.gloss: to participate in (something)
to belong to, to pertain to, to be included among
to be newly introduced [with عَلَى (ʕalā) ‘into something’] (e.g. of a factor, aspect, element, etc.)
to be prefixed
to begin (of an event or occurrence)
to be diseased, to be abnormal
to make enter, to bring in, to let in
to enter, to insert, to include
income
revenues, receipts, returns
interference, intervention
doubt, misgiving
disturbance, imbalance, derangement, disorder, mental defect
defect, infirmity
